calendar.matters:
Petition For Confirmation Of Successor Trustee; To Remove Trust From Continued Court Supervision; And To Unblock Blocked Account
Off Calendar Apr-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Frank Russow appearing via zoom for Olga Ortega (successor trustee).
Gonzalo Gutierrez appearing via zoom for Elizabeth Mena (beneficiary).
Trisha D. Friedeberg appearing via zoom for and with Shirley Basch (Petitioner) via zoom.

No one was present to object to petition. Mr. Russo stated that they reached an agreement, that it was passed along yesterday and that he is waiting to get confirmation. The petition for confirmation of successor trustee, etc. is under submission for review of the order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Maloney. Not reported.

Petition For Confirmation Of Successor Trustee; To Remove Trust From Continued Court Supervision; And To Unblock Blocked Account
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Continued To Mar-29-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
TO ADVANCE HEARING DATE ON PETITION FOR CONFIRMATION OF SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE; TO REMOVE TRUST FROM CONTINUED COURT SUPERVISION; AND TO UNBLOCK BLOCKED ACCOUNT

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Fdic Insurance Of The Blocked Account And Proof That Ms. Abeyta Exercised Her Power Of Appointment
Off Calendar Jan-10-2022 Off Calendar

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Trisha Friedeberg, Esq. for Shirley Basch;
Frank Russow, Esq. for Olga Ortega, Trustee.

Ms. Friedeberg confirms for the Court that she is fine with the funds being in the blocked account with the exception of the funds all being in the Survivor's Trust. The Court stated that it had reviewed the papers, that it is appropriate to waive notice as requested by Ms. Friedeberg, and that it is prepared to take the matter under submission. Ms. Friedeberg states that there was no objection noting that they have come to an agreement. Mr. Russow states that the survivor's beneficiary has been located in San Jose, Costa Rica. Ms. Friedeberg requests that the funds remain in blocked accounts until Ms. Cordova is reached.

The Court orders Mr. Russow to file a complete copy of the seller's closing statement. Mr. Russow is also to provide a copy of Lucy Abeyta's exercise of her power appointment. The Court advises Mr. Russow stating that he needs to help his client transfer funds into the Exemption Trust.

Mr. Russow states that he can get the documents on file this week. The Court set status on the filing of the seller's closing statement and a copy of Lucy Abeyta's exercise of her power of appointment for Monday, January 10, 2022, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Mr. Russow states that the funds are to stay in a blocked account, that they are to be distributed to the Exemption Trust and Survivor's Trust, and that there is an outstanding burial bill. Ms. Friedeberg states that they can file an ex parte to make the funeral payment.

The Court took the Second Amended Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustees; for Proof of Blocked Account, Inventory and Trust Amendments, for Continued Court Supervision and for Funding under submission. The Court strikes the following language in the proposed order: no additional order will be necessary to unblock funds.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: R. Loo. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Fdic Insurance Of The Blocked Account And Proof That Ms. Abeyta Exercised Her Power Of Appointment
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Continued To Jan-10-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-27-2021 Continued To Nov-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frank Russow, via ZOOM, for Olga Ortega, Trustee; Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, for Shirley Basch, Petitioner.

The court at the outset asks Ms. Friedberg regarding the delivery of courtesy copies to the court. Ms. Friedeberg reports that courtesy copies had been delivered shortly after filing and then once again a short time ago. The court then queries counsel regarding why nothing has been filed to confirm that the more than $1 million dollars has been separated into multiple FDIC insured accounts. Mr. Russow reports that he has sent five emails to the bank manager at Chase Bank but has not received a reply. The court then asks counsel whether there is a settlement in principle. Mr. Russow confirms and asks for 30 days to provide an accounting and that the only item remaining is to provide an Inventory and Appraisal. Ms. Friedeberg then advises the court that there might be an issue with lack of notice to a beneficiary in Costa Rica. Her larger concern is completing this matter and requests that her client be appointed temporary trustee to move this case along. Mr. Russow advises that he believes that his client can finish this matter as Trustee and the main reason for the delay is that his client is elderly and difficulties in obtaining records which had previously burned in a fire. Mr. Russow also confirms that attempts have been made to locate the possible beneficiary in Costa Rica. Ms. Friedeberg expresses concerns regarding the amount of time her client has waited for this matter to be resolved and with the uninsured funds.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Appointment of Shirley Basch as Successor Trustee, and Status of FDIC insurance, etc., are continued to Nov-22-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: E. Prado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for and with Shirley Basch by zoom.
Frank Russow appeared by zoom for Olga Ortega (successor trustee).

Mr. Russow stated that on August 19th they finally received written confirmation that the account is blocked, that he e-mailed it to Ms. Friedeberg and that he e-filed it with the court, but it got rejected because of the size of the image. The court noted that the account is in excess of the FDIC insured limit. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she would like to see the statements and that she needs to be able to notice the beneficiaries of the survivor's trust. Mr. Russow stated that he will meet with Ms. Ortega to get that information and if not, he will check with the American Consulate to see who that person/beneficiary is, and spoke of Ms. Abeyta's power of appointment having been exercised. Ms. Friedeberg questions whether the amendment to the trust needs to be filed. Mr. Russow stated that he will file the power of appointment tomorrow and that he believes the beneficiary is Elizabeth Mena Cordoza. Status of the blocked account is off calendar. Status of FDIC insurance of the blocked account and proof that Ms. Abeyta exercised her power of appointment is set for September 27, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-09-2021 Continued To Sep-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-09-2021 Continued To Aug-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, for Shirley Basch, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Frank Russow, via ZOOM, for Olga Ortega, Successor Trustee.

The court notes the pending second amended petition in the court's e-filing queue. Ms. Friedeberg confirms and also requests that the petition to approve settlement agreement be heard ex parte in lieu of a noticed petition. The court then requests specifics of the agreement and advises counsel that the petition for settlement agreement should be filed as a noticed petition. Ms. Freideberg reports that she has requested proof that funds have been deposited into a blocked account but has yet to receive any. Mr. Russow reports that the property has sold and that his client received a check and had taken the check to the financial institution but that the funds had been deposited into a non-blocked account. Mr. Russow reports that he has reached-out to the financial institution in order to open a blocked account and transfer the funds to that account but that he has had issues in doing so but that in his most recent communication with the bank, he was advised that the bank's legal section would need to review this matter and that he would receive notification from them in a week. Ms. Friedeberg reports that she has no issues with Mr. Russow's attempts to open the blocked account but believes that his client is not capable to act as Trustee due to her failure to comply with her duties as Trustee. Mr. Russow reports that the pending settlement agreement addresses Ms. Friedeberg's concerns. Ms. Friedeberg requests that the court set the Petition that is pending in the court's e-fling queue on the same date as the next calling of this hearing. The court advises that it will not consider the petition on the next calling of this matter but, the clerk's office will be asked to file the pending petition even though it shows today's date as the hearing date.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee is continued to August 23, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status of Blocked Account is set for the same date.

***After further consideration by the court, the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee is continued to September 27, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-08-2021 Continued To May-03-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for and with Shirley Basch by zoom.
Frank Russow appeared by zoom for Olga Ortega (Trustee).

Mr. Russow stated that escrow closed, that they gave wire instructions for the money to go into the blocked account at Chase, and that the money was wired, but he does not have a statement of the account showing that the money was there. Mr. Russow also stated that he sent a copy of the closing statement to Ms. Friedeberg, that the property sold for $300,000.00 more than the date of death value and that they need to file tax returns. In addition, Mr. Russow asked that the blocked account be unblocked so that they can have funds to hire an Accountant to do the taxes and pay the taxes. Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-08-2021 Continued To Mar-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for and with Shirley Basch by zoom
Frank Russow appeared by zoom for Lucy Abeyta (Former Trustee) and Olga Ortega (Temporary Trustee).

Mr. Russow informed the Court that Ms. Abeyta passed away on February 5, 2021, and stated that there was a calendaring oversight and he did not submit the joint status report. Mr. Russow also stated that twenty offers came in on the property, that they took the highest offer of $1,133,000.00, that escrow was supposed to close last Friday and that they anticipate escrow closing this Friday. Mr. Russow also stated that Ms. Ortega opened a blocked account, and that they are waiting for close of escrow and cutting of the check. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she agrees to a short continuance of two to four weeks. The Petition for Appointment of Shirley Basch as Successor Trustee is continued to March 8, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Continued To Feb-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg, Esq., counsel for and with Shirley Basch, Petitioner (appearing by video conference); Frank Russow, Esq., counsel for Lucy Abeyta, Trustee (appearing by telephone).

Mr. Russow advises the Court that his client has been determined to be unable to continue as trustee and that pursuant to a durable Power of Attorney, her sister, Olga Ortega, has acted to put the real property on the market for sale. Ms. Friedeberg contends that the durable Power of Attorney is not sufficient to fill the de facto vacancy of the trusteeship, that Ms. Ortega does not have authority to sell the property, that it is not clear whom Mr. Russow represents as his client is no longer competent to serve as trustee, and that her client is very concerned about how the proceeds from the sale of the property will be accounted for. The Court notes the sale process as reported by Mr. Russow seems appropriate if not procedurally correct. After further discussion, the Court orders the removal of Lucy Abeyta as trustee, appoints Olga Ortega as trustee but with limited authority only to sell the real property. The Court further orders that the proceeds from the sale be placed in a blocked account pending further hearing after the sale.

Ms. Friedeberg would request that the sale be subject to Court confirmation and that the trust be placed under Court supervision. After discussion with counsel, the Court declines the requests but requires Mr. Russow to communicate with Ms. Friedeberg in a timely manner on Ms. Orgeta's activities as Limited Successor Trustee, particularly with respect to all aspects of the sale of the property.

The Court continues the matter to February 8, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. and for status of the sale.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Continued To Dec-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Trisha Friedeberg for and with Shirley Basch, Petitioner.
Frank Russow for Trustee.

The Court noted that Mr. Russow requested a continuance to this petition which Ms. Friedeberg objected to. Ms. Friedeberg stated it has been 120 days since mediation was ordered without any progress, that there is no trustee acting as Mr. Russow's client has dementia, that property taxes were not paid for a time, that a temporary successor trustee should be appointed until a professional fiduciary is appointed, that the promissory note done one year after date of death was done incorrectly, and asked the Court to appoint a successor trustee. Mr. Russow stated there was a fire to the property in 2017, that the house is rebuilt as new condition right now, all expenses and maintenance are up to date, that he got one of the two physicians' letters, that the July 2018 document designated his client's sister, Olga Ortega, as the successor trustee. Mr. Russow further stated that the only asset is the house which needs to be sold as his client does not have enough liquid assets to maintain the house, that his client is 91 years old now and needs to sell the house to pay for her assited living home to live out her life. The Court stated that Mr. Russow has not been having good success to get the 2nd letter from the physician and that it is better to appoint a successor trustee than to leave things status quo. Mr. Russow stated that they are one letter away from the successor trustee being appointed, that he has already discussed with the real estate agent who thinks the house is ready to list and will be a quick sale. Ms. Friedeberg stated that it is a major issue that Mr. Russow does not have a client to give him direction.

The Amended Petition for Appointment of Shirley Basch as Successor Trustee and to Direct Funding of Trust is continued to December 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee

Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-08-2020 Continued To Sep-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frank Russow appeared by telephone for Lucy Abeyta (Trustee).
Trisha Friedeberg and Will Doyle appeared by telephone for Shirley Basch.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that she believes they delivered a courtesy copy of the response quite some time ago and that she also delivered a copy yesterday. The Court asks if the Parties have explored other options to ensure that the property is being properly maintained. Mr. Russow stated that mediation would be extremely difficult on his Client as she is elderly and very emotional, that the property is being cared for, that all of the expenses are being paid and that there are no issues with the property. Ms. Friedeberg stated that this is not the basis for their petition, that they had been asking for the property taxes to be cleared up, that it did not happen until they filed the petition and that they have asked for proof of funding of all of the trusts. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that the point of this is to address issues that will later be unfixable (improper funding). Mr. Russow stated that the property will have to be sold, that Ms. Abeyta no longer has money to pay for her needs, that a trial will be extremely harmful to his Client and that he is open to mediation. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she is agreeable to mediation. The Court signed an order referring the matter to pro bono mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 8, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 9, 2020. The Petition for Appointment of Shirley Basch as Successor Trustee is continued to and the Parties are to return on September 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
